patches.find: Reaching the target of <20 km/yr by 2027 would require ~18 km/yr/yr deceleration — roughly 23× the historical average of ~0.77 km/yr/yr.
patches.replace: The dome's formula predicts ~21 km/yr for 2027 and ~19 km/yr for 2028 — meaning the dome's own model does not even predict reaching <20 km/yr until mid-2028. Meanwhile the observed rate remains ~38 km/yr, already 40% above the formula's output.
patches.rationale: ISS-928: '<20 km/yr by 2027' cannot be found in dome's published material. Dome formula gives 21 km/yr for 2027 (above 20). The TLDR was critiquing a target the dome never stated. Replace with what the dome's formula actually predicts.

patches.find: dome predicts the North Magnetic Pole (NMP) drift rate will decelerate to <20 km/yr by 2027.
patches.replace: dome predicts the North Magnetic Pole (NMP) drift rate will decelerate, following its formula: rate = 55×exp(−0.08×(year−2015)) km/yr. This gives ~21 km/yr for 2027 and ~19 km/yr for 2028.
patches.rationale: ISS-928: Body text opens with '<20 km/yr by 2027' claim not sourced to any dome publication. Replace with the dome's actual formula output, which is ~21 km/yr for 2027.
